common.disclaimer: This calculator is for educational purposes only. Please consult a financial advisor for investment decisions.

ppf.title: PPF Calculator 2025-26
ppf.metaTitle: PPF Calculator 2025-26 - Calculate PPF Maturity Amount | FinCalc India
ppf.metaDescription: Calculate your PPF maturity amount, interest earned and year-wise growth. Updated for FY 2025-26 with current PPF rate of 7.1%.

faq.a1: Public Provident Fund (PPF) is a long-term savings scheme backed by the Government of India with a 15-year lock-in period and tax benefits under Section 80C.
faq.q2: What is the current PPF interest rate?
faq.a2: The current PPF interest rate for Q1 FY2025-26 is 7.1% per annum, compounded annually.
faq.a3: No. PPF enjoys EEE (Exempt-Exempt-Exempt) status - deposits, interest, and maturity amount are all tax-free.
faq.q4: Can I extend PPF after 15 years?
faq.a4: Yes, you can extend PPF in blocks of 5 years after the initial 15-year period, with or without further contributions.
faq.q5: When can I make partial withdrawals?
faq.a5: Partial withdrawals are allowed from the 7th year onwards, up to 50% of the balance at the end of the 4th preceding year.

fd.title: FD Calculator 2025-26
fd.metaTitle: Fixed Deposit Calculator 2025-26 - Calculate FD Maturity Amount | FinCalc India
fd.metaDescription: Calculate FD maturity amount, interest earned with compound interest. Compare rates across major Indian banks for 2025-26.

faq.q1: What is the difference between cumulative and non-cumulative FD?
faq.a1: In cumulative FD, interest is compounded and paid at maturity. In non-cumulative FD, interest is paid out at regular intervals.
faq.q2: Which banks offer the highest FD rates?
faq.a2: Small finance banks and some private banks typically offer higher rates than PSU banks. Rates vary by tenure and amount.
faq.a3: Yes, FD interest is taxable as per your income tax slab. TDS is deducted if interest exceeds ₹40,000 per year (₹50,000 for senior citizens).

tax.title: Income Tax Calculator 2025-26
tax.metaTitle: Income Tax Calculator 2025-26 - New vs Old Regime | FinCalc India
tax.metaDescription: Calculate income tax under both new and old regime for FY 2025-26. Find which tax regime saves you more money.
tax.recommendation: Based on your inputs, {regime} is better for you

faq.q1: Which tax regime should I choose?
faq.a1: Choose new regime if you have minimal deductions. Choose old regime if you have significant deductions under 80C, HRA, home loan interest, etc.
faq.q2: What is the standard deduction in new regime?
faq.a2: The standard deduction in new regime is ₹75,000 for FY 2025-26.
faq.q3: Can I switch between tax regimes?
faq.a3: Yes, salaried individuals can switch between regimes every year. Business owners can switch but with certain restrictions.
